findings.title: Snap gate now fires on the WMA-lagged point: 18-40px refixations lose instant snap entirely, larger saccades land in 3 hops â€” contradicts the commit's '~zero added latency' claim
findings.scenario: Patient reads YouTube search results and shifts gaze from one link to the next, ~30px below. Before 9dc3c44 the page cursor snapped to the new link in one frame (~15ms) and the page-side dwell onset began immediately. After it, the first forwarded frame moves only 0.45 x 30 = 13.5px (under the 18px snap gate), so the cursor chases the target through the alpha-0.9 EMA over ~4 frames (~60ms) before the 1.5px jitter gate parks it. Every small refixation now starts its 280ms dwell onset ~45-60ms later and the cursor visibly rubber-bands behind quick glances. For big saccades (e.g. video player to sidebar, ~700px) the 'snap' lands at 45% of the jump, then 75%, then 100% â€” a 3-hop sweep over ~30-45ms instead of one clean jump, briefly lighting hover highlights on cards the patient never looked at (card snap-in zone is 130px). No misclick is possible (sweep << onsetMs 280) and dwellMs/onsetMs configs are unchanged, so this is a latency/visual regression, not a selection failure.
findings.evidence: The WMA is computed BEFORE the distance check, and the snap compares and snaps to the WMA output, not the gaze point: 'const raw = (w1 && w2) ? { x: gazeNow.x * 0.45 + w1.x * 0.30 + w2.x * 0.25, ... }' (line 3809-3813), then 'const dx = raw.x - prev.x; ... if (dist > 18) { smoothedGazeRef.current = { x: raw.x, y: raw.y }; browser.updateGazeCursor(raw.x, raw.y, ...)' (lines 3824-3832). For a true gaze step of D with the cursor parked at the old fixation, frame-1 dist = 0.45D, so the >18px gate needs D > 40px (previously D > 18px). Steps in (18px, 40px] â€” the typical adjacent-link distance â€” fall into the EMA branch ('const alpha = dist > 8 ? 0.9 : 0.74', line 3842); since the WMA input itself converges only at frame 3 (0.45D, 0.75D, 1.0D), the rendered point reaches within 1.5px of target at frame ~4 (~60ms at 66Hz) instead of frame 1. The commit message and docs/EYE_TRACKING_CHANGES.md Entry 10 both claim 'Causal, ~zero added lag at 66Hz' â€” false for any move in the 18-70px range. There is no indefinite 'slow crawl' (the alpha-0.74 branch needs first-frame dist <= 8, i.e. D <= 17.8px, which the old code also never snapped), so severity is minor, but the behavior change is real and traceable.

findings.title: WMA has no gaze-gap reset: after a tracker dropout, up to 55% of the stale pre-gap position blends into the first re-acquired frames, sweeping the page cursor through screen regions the patient never looked at
findings.scenario: Patient is on the YouTube grid, gaze on a top-left card. The Tobii drops tracking for a moment (blink, look at caregiver â€” the page side itself treats >150ms gaps as real via gapPauseMs=150, and the project baseline notes ET5 gaps of 100-500ms+). During the gap no forward() runs, so wmaPrev1/2 freeze holding the top-left samples. Patient re-fixates on the bottom-right 'Next' control; on the first re-acquired frame raw = 0.45*new + 0.55*old â€” a point in the MIDDLE of the screen, where dist > 18 makes the code SNAP the visible cursor to it, then hop again at 75% before reaching the true point on frame 3. Before 9dc3c44, the first post-gap frame was raw gaze and the cursor snapped directly to where the patient is actually looking. Now every blink-plus-refixation produces a 2-3 frame (~30-45ms) ghost traverse through unrelated content, which can flash the hover highlight on an intermediate card (130px snap-in zone). It cannot produce a click (onsetMs=280 >> 45ms, and gapPause/progress-retention guard the dwell clocks), so impact is a visible cursor artifact, not a misfire.
findings.evidence: forward() is purely event-driven ('const unsub = ws.subscribeGaze(() => forward())', line 3854) and the WMA history is only cleared on hide paths (!ige branch lines 3784-3785, toolbar branch lines 3801-3802) and on effect re-run (lines 3767-3768). There is no time check anywhere in the prefilter: 'const raw = (w1 && w2) ? { x: gazeNow.x * 0.45 + w1.x * 0.30 + w2.x * 0.25, ... }' (lines 3809-3813) blends samples regardless of whether they are 15ms or 5s old. The commit message's safety claim â€” 'Filter state resets whenever the cursor hides or the browser session reopens' â€” is satisfied for hides (I verified every hide path resets both refs), but a stream gap hides nothing: the cursor stays parked, the refs stay stale, and the matching page side explicitly recognizes >150ms gaps as a distinct condition ('gapPauseMs: 150', electron/browser/browserGazeController.ts:171) while this renderer-side filter does not. Pre-change behavior for the same gap was a single-frame snap to true gaze (dist = |gazeNow - prev| > 18 with raw = gazeNow), so the intermediate points are new. A gap-age check resetting wmaPrev1/2 (mirroring the 150ms threshold) would restore the old post-gap snap.
